name: Alpha Futures
description_short: Alpha Futures is a UK-based futures prop firm offering Standard, Advanced, and Zero plans with EOD trailing drawdown, 90% profit splits, and weekly payouts after five winning days.
description_long:
Alpha Futures is a London-area proprietary trading firm founded in April 2024 by George David Kohler. Operated by Alpha Futures Limited (UK Companies House #15655643) from a Harpenden, Hertfordshire office, the firm has built an unusually strong early reputation — a 4.9/5 Trustpilot rating across 2,500+ verified reviews places it ahead of most established US competitors on trader satisfaction.

The product line covers three distinct evaluation paths. The Standard plan is the entry-level option at $79/month for $50K, $159/month for $100K, or $239/month for $150K, each with a $149 one-time activation fee. Standard accounts start at a 70% profit split and scale to 90% after five approved payouts. The Advanced plan is more expensive — $139/month for $50K, $279/month for $100K, $419/month for $150K — but pays a flat 90% split from the first withdrawal with no consistency rule on the qualified phase. The Zero plan is Alpha's instant-funding product, priced at $79–$239/month with no activation fee, and skips the evaluation entirely with a 90% split.

Risk management is Alpha's core pitch. The Maximum Loss Limit is calculated end-of-day on every account, trails upward at session close only, and locks at starting balance — it will never trigger intraday on a wick or news spike. That trader-friendly structure is one reason the firm polls so well on Trustpilot. Consistency rules apply during evaluation (50% on Standard/Advanced, none on Zero) and qualified traders receive bi-weekly payouts on Standard or weekly payouts on Advanced/Zero after five winning days with at least $200 profit each. Processing is 48 business hours via ACH, Wire, SWIFT, Wise, or Rise, with a $1,000 minimum and $15,000 per-request cap.

Supported platforms are Tradovate, NinjaTrader, Quantower, TradingView (through Tradovate), plus Alpha's proprietary Deepchart and Deepdom order-flow tools — platform choice locks at purchase. Automated trading is a significant limitation: Expert Advisors, bots, and high-frequency strategies are prohibited across every product, which rules Alpha out for algo-focused traders. News trading is unrestricted during evaluation and on Advanced qualified accounts, but Standard and Zero qualified accounts enforce a two-minute buffer before and after high-impact events. The affiliate program pays 15% recurring commission with weekly payouts.

international traders needing Wise or Rise payouts
